dialogos.texto_en: (Is that building over there the Oceanic Culture Museum?\n Is it like an actual museum?)
Ah, Uesugi-kun.
You came over here, huh.\nI didn't notice because we were inside the building.
Hey.\nSo, did you go see something else already?
Yes, I just finished\nvisiting the Oceanic Culture Museum.
Oh, I see.\nWhat did it have?
The exhibit showcased the history and materials\nof how ancient people crossed the sea\nand spread across new lands.
There were various tools and canoes\nused by people in ancient times on display.\nIt was really quite impressive.
The way the ships were constructed\nwas explained like in a manga,\nso it was easy to understand, and really interesting.
I see.
(This vague explanation\n sounds like something Yotsuba would say...)
It was also fun to trace the history\nof how people crossed the sea and spread out.
Yeah, around Micronesia or Polynesia, right?
As expected of you, Uesugi-kun, you know a lot.
Even so%p-2;――
It's amazing that people crossed the vast ocean\nin an age of undeveloped technology to seek new lands.\nTheir courage is quite impressive.
Searching for new lands beyond the horizon, huh?\nTruly, the bravery of people from the past is admirable.
We could learn from their courage.
I'll pass.\nI have confidence\nthat I wouldn't be the first to give up.
Even if you were shipwrecked,\nyou might end up stranded on some island, you know?
I don't want to test my luck like that...
In Okinawa, it is believed that there is a paradise\ncalled 'Nirai Kanai' where the gods reside across the sea.
If you're lucky, you might be able to reach it.
Apparently, at the beginning of each year,\nthe gods would visit Okinawa and bestows various blessings.
Like when you receive New Year's money, huh?
Really, now...\nIf you mock faith, you'll be punished, you know?
Yeah, sorry.\nIn other words, it means that people in Okinawa\nare receiving blessings from the sea.
Indeed, for people in Okinawa,\nthe sea that bestows blessings is very important.
After all, it's an island surrounded by the sea.\nThe people probably turned\ntheir respect for the sea into faith.
But, the idea of a paradise is mysterious, isn't it?
Well, it's a place that becomes an object of faith.\nJust the fact that gods live there\nis already quite mysterious.
No, that's not what I meant.
Even if everyone has their own ideals,\nis there really a place where everyone can be happy?
Well, I don't think such a place exists.
So, why do people believe in it?
If you don't believe that such a place exists, \nthere would be times when you're unable \nto continue when the going gets tough.
Yes, indeed.
But isn't that just escaping from reality?
*sigh*\nIt's still difficult for everyone to be happy,\nisn't it?
(...Could this be Yotsuba?)
It's probably something like a dream.
A...dream?
Just thinking about wanting to make a dream come true\ncan become the driving force to move forward, you know?
Whether or not there is a utopia in reality\ndoesn't matter. Perhaps it's believing in it\nthat's important.
...That might be true.
*sigh*\nIt's still difficult for everyone to be happy,\nisn't it?
(...Could this be Yotsuba?)
Even if you think it might not be possible,\nyou can't do your best if you give up from the start.
And believing that you can get a little closer, if you try.\n...I'm sure that's how it is.
...I think I understand, a little.
It's like a goal that you can't see,\nbut you keep running, so you don't stop.
*sigh*\nIt's still difficult for everyone to be happy,\nisn't it?
(...Could this be Yotsuba?)
Certainly, not everything can go as smoothly as we want.
If ideals are different for everyone,\nfinding the greatest common divisor of ideals\nmight be the best approach.
Oooohh...
Hm? What happened, suddenly?
I'm in trouble...\nMaybe I used my brain too much,\nbut I suddenly feel hungry...
...What the? You got me worried there.
It's your fault, you know?\nBringing up topics like greatest common divisor,\nused in studying!
...Hold on, wait a minute.\nIs that really my fault?
This isn't good!\nI need to replenish my blood sugar right now!
I remember there was a gelato shop nearby.\nLet's take a break and eat something delicious!
Well then, let's set sail\nin search of new gelato we haven't tried yet!
O-okay.\nLet's go.
(I wonder if even the ancient people\n who crossed the sea a long time ago\n set off with such enthusiasm...?)
